meta.title: Enterprise AGI/ASI Governance Master Framework (2026-2030)
meta.subtitle: Institutional-grade, regulator-ready AGI/ASI and enterprise AI governance frameworks, reference architectures, safety and containment protocols, financial-services model risk management, civilizational-scale compute oversight, and implementation roadmaps for Fortune 500, Global 2000, and G-SIFIs.
meta.classification: CONFIDENTIAL — Board / C-Suite / Prudential Supervisor / Treaty Authority / Internal & External Audit
meta.owner: Group Chief AI Officer (CAIO) — co-signed by CRO, CISO, GC, COO
audience: Board of Directors / Risk & Audit Committees
C-Suite (CEO, CFO, CRO, CISO, CAIO, CTO, GC, COO)
Group Heads of Model Risk, Enterprise Risk, Compliance
Prudential & conduct supervisors (PRA, FCA, OCC, Fed, ECB, MAS, HKMA, BaFin, FINMA)
Data protection authorities (ICO, CNIL, EDPB), CFPB
EU AI Act notified bodies, ISO/IEC 42001 certifiers
Enterprise architects, AI platform engineers, researchers
regulatoryAlignment: EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) — Annex III, Annex IV, Art. 9/10/12/13/14/15, Art. 53/55 GPAI
NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0 + GenAI Profile (AI 600-1)
US FCRA / ECOA / Reg B / CFPB UDAAP
SR 11-7 / OCC 2011-12 / PRA SS1/23 — Model Risk Management
PRA SS2/21 (Outsourcing); FCA Consumer Duty; FCA AI Update 2024
MAS FEAT principles + Veritas toolkit; HKMA HLP on Big Data & AI
EO 14110, OMB M-24-10, US AI Bill of Rights blueprint

horizonMilestones.2026Q2: EU AI Act Art. 6 high-risk obligations enforcement
horizonMilestones.2026Q3: MV-AGI governance stack mandatory for systemic banks
horizonMilestones.2027Q4: ISO/IEC 42001 certification expected of all G-SIFIs
horizonMilestones.2030Q1: Frontier compute governance treaty (GAGCOT) in force

executiveSummary.purpose: To provide a single, regulator-ready, board-approvable master framework that unifies enterprise AI, agentic-AI, AGI/ASI containment, and civilizational compute oversight into one audit-traceable governance system aligned with all major global regulatory regimes.
executiveSummary.scope: Spans all AI systems across the enterprise — from high-risk credit/trading models to autonomous agents and frontier general-purpose AI — with extensions to inter-firm and treaty-level oversight.
designPrinciples: Compliance-as-code: every policy is enforceable in CI/CD and runtime
Human-on-the-loop with kinetic tripwires for irreversibility
Bias-aware fairness across protected classes (FCRA/ECOA, GDPR Art. 22)
Formal alignment metrics with PID-based drift control
Treaty-ready: artefacts portable to ICGC and supervisory colleges

keyOutcomes.boardReportingCadence: Quarterly with monthly KRI exception packs

executiveSummary.boardNarrative: This master framework converts AI governance from a fragmented control set into an integrated risk-bearing capital function. Capital, conduct, and existential-safety risks are jointly modelled, enabling the Board to approve AI strategy with the same rigour applied to credit, market, and operational risk.

M1_pillars.title: M1 — Multilayered AI Governance Pillars (G1-G7)
M1_pillars.summary: Seven pillars define the institutional governance topology, from board accountability down to autonomous-agent guardrails.

pillars.name: Board & Strategic Oversight
pillars.objective: Risk appetite, strategic AI bets, capital allocation

pillars.name: Executive Accountability
pillars.objective: Single accountable executive with veto + kill-switch authority

pillars.name: Model Risk Management (MRM)
pillars.owner: Group Head of Model Risk (2nd LoD)
pillars.objective: Independent validation, ongoing monitoring, MV report

pillars.name: Data, Privacy & Fairness
pillars.objective: Lawful basis, minimisation, fairness across protected classes

pillars.name: Compliance & Conduct
pillars.objective: Regulatory mapping, conduct outcomes, customer fairness

pillars.name: Frontier / Civilizational Risk
pillars.objective: GPAI Art. 53/55, ICGC reporting, AGI containment readiness

M2_regulatory.title: M2 — Regulatory Alignment Matrix (16 Axes)
M2_regulatory.summary: Cross-walk of every governance control to its regulatory anchor.

rows.keyArticles: Human rights, democracy, rule of law

M3_architectures.title: M3 — Enterprise Reference Architectures
M3_architectures.summary: Nine production-grade architectures composing the enterprise AI estate.

architectures.name: Sentinel AI Governance Platform v2.4
architectures.purpose: Unified runtime containment, telemetry, kill-switch, kinetic tripwire

architectures.name: WorkflowAI Pro (WP-033)
architectures.purpose: Governed agentic workflow + prompt lifecycle platform

architectures.name: Enterprise AI Interoperability Profile (EAIP)
architectures.purpose: Cross-vendor governance interchange — policy, evidence, telemetry envelopes

architectures.name: High-Assurance RAG Platform
architectures.purpose: Retrieval-augmented generation with governance-grade citation, lineage, and PII redaction

architectures.name: Governed Agentic Workflows
architectures.purpose: Multi-agent orchestration with constitutional guardrails and canary deploys

architectures.name: Kafka WORM Audit Logging Cluster
architectures.purpose: Immutable, PQC-signed, hash-chained AI telemetry for 7-year SEC retention

architectures.name: Docker Swarm + Kubernetes Hardened Runtime
architectures.purpose: Workload isolation, mTLS service mesh, signed images, runtime attestation

architectures.name: Node.js / Python Governance Sidecars

sections.title: Governance Standards for Hyperparameter Control
controls: Hyperparameter changes are version-controlled (Git, signed commits)
Material hyperparameter changes (Δlearning-rate >50%, depth ±2 layers, regulariser swap) trigger IMV re-validation
Random-seed pinning + deterministic CUDA flags for reproducibility (within hardware tolerance)
Hyperparameter sweep results retained in WORM with cost & energy attribution
Production hyperparameters require 2-of-3 approval (1LoD model owner, 2LoD validator, change advisory board)
Rollback hyperparameter set always pinned and tested in canary lane

M4_safety.title: M4 — AGI/ASI Safety & Containment Frameworks
M4_safety.summary: Eight protocols spanning institutional safety, frontier alignment, and civilizational hedges.

protocols.name: Luminous Engine Codex
protocols.purpose: Codex of inviolable constitutional principles for frontier systems

protocols.name: Cognitive Resonance Protocol (CRP)
protocols.purpose: Continuous alignment-resonance scoring with PID drift control

protocols.name: Omni-Sentinel Multi-Modal Filter
protocols.purpose: Vision/audio/code multi-modal containment with adversarial robustness

protocols.name: MV-AGI Governance Stack (Minimum-Viable)
protocols.purpose: Smallest auditable AGI governance layer required pre-deployment
protocols.scope: Any system >1e25 FLOP or with autonomy ≥L3

protocols.name: Crisis Simulation Programme (GC1-GC7)
protocols.purpose: Tabletop + live-fire crisis exercises across institution / treaty axes

protocols.name: Frontier Risk Taxonomy (FRT)
protocols.purpose: Catalogue of catastrophic & existential failure modes with leading indicators

protocols.name: Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP/RSDP)
protocols.purpose: Capability-conditional commitments triggering pause / red-team / disclosure

scenarios.name: Cross-border capability shock
scenarios.trigger: Frontier model exceeds eval threshold mid-deploy

M7_kafkaGac.title: M7 — Kafka ACL Governance & Continuous Compliance Engine
M7_kafkaGac.summary: Terraform-based governance-as-code with WORM evidence, OPA gates, and auditor workflows.

sections.title: Kafka ACL Governance Pattern
components: Continuous ACL drift detection (cron job → OPA → ticket)
Quarterly ACL recertification by data owner

sections.title: WORM Evidence Storage
design: S3 Object Lock (compliance mode) — 7-year retention (SR 11-7 / SEC 17a-4(f))
Daily Merkle-root anchored to public timestamping (RFC 3161 + blockchain anchor)

modules.outputs: Maps evidence to control IDs (240+ controls)

modules: tf-aws-kms-cmk-rotated — annual rotation, key policy with break-glass

sections.title: CI/CD Integration (GitHub Actions)
stages: Container build + SLSA provenance + Cosign sign

sections.title: Auditor Workflow

Evidence query UI: control → evidence → proof chain
Export to PDF/A-3 with embedded JSON-LD evidence
Findings logged to WORM Kafka topic for traceability

codeExamples.terraformKafkaAcls:
# tf-confluent-acls — per-topic ACL bundle
resource "confluent_kafka_acl" "telemetry_writer" {
  kafka_cluster { id = var.cluster_id }
  resource_type = "TOPIC"
  resource_name = "ai.telemetry.v1"
  pattern_type  = "LITERAL"
  principal     = "User:sa-sentinel-emitter"
  host          = "*"
  operation     = "WRITE"
  permission    = "ALLOW"
}

resource "confluent_kafka_acl" "telemetry_audit_reader" {
  kafka_cluster { id = var.cluster_id }
  resource_type = "TOPIC"
  resource_name = "ai.telemetry.v1"
  pattern_type  = "LITERAL"
  principal     = "User:sa-auditor"
  host          = "*"
  operation     = "READ"
  permission    = "ALLOW"
}
codeExamples.merkleAuditPython:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Daily Merkle-root WORM audit (EAGV)."""
import hashlib, json, time, boto3
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import ed25519

def merkle(leaves):
    if not leaves: return b""
    layer = [hashlib.sha256(l).digest() for l in leaves]
    while len(layer) > 1:
        if len(layer) % 2: layer.append(layer[-1])
        layer = [hashlib.sha256(layer[i]+layer[i+1]).digest()
                 for i in range(0,len(layer),2)]
    return layer[0]

def daily_audit(bucket, prefix, signing_key):
    s3 = boto3.client("s3")
    leaves = []
    for o in s3.list_objects_v2(Bucket=bucket, Prefix=prefix).get("Contents", []):
        body = s3.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=o["Key"])["Body"].read()
        leaves.append(body)
    root = merkle(leaves)
    sig = signing_key.sign(root)
    manifest = {"date": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%d"),
                "merkleRoot": root.hex(),
                "signature": sig.hex(),
                "leafCount": len(leaves)}
    s3.put_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=f"{prefix}/_manifests/{manifest['date']}.json",
                  Body=json.dumps(manifest).encode(),
                  ObjectLockMode="COMPLIANCE",
                  ObjectLockRetainUntilDate=time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))
    return manifest
codeExamples.ciGithubActions:
# .github/workflows/eagv-pipeline.yml
name: eagv-pipeline
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  govern:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Lint rego
        run: opa fmt --diff policies/ && opa test policies/
      - name: Conftest gates
        run: conftest test --policy policies deploy/
      - name: Adversarial suite
        run: pytest tests/adversarial -q
      - name: Mechanistic audit
        run: python tools/circuit_scanner.py --threshold 0.92
      - name: Build + SLSA + Cosign
        run: |
          docker build -t app:${{ github.sha }} .
          cosign sign --yes app:${{ github.sha }}
      - name: Sigstore attest
        run: cosign attest --predicate evidence.json app:${{ github.sha }}
      - name: Canary deploy
        run: kubectl apply -f deploy/canary-5pct.yaml
codeExamples.regulatorReportTemplate:
<!-- Markdown report template -->
<title>Annex IV Technical Documentation — Model {{modelId}}</title>
<abstract>
Regulator-ready dossier covering EU AI Act Art. 11 + Annex IV for the
high-risk AI system {{modelId}} operated by {{operator}}.
</abstract>
<content>

## 1. General description
- Intended purpose: {{purpose}}
- Provider / deployer: {{provider}} / {{deployer}}
- Versions covered: {{versions}}

## 2. Detailed description
- Architecture, training data, validation methodology
- Logging (Art. 12) and human oversight (Art. 14)

## 3. Risk management (Art. 9)
- Hazard identification, evaluation, mitigations

## 4. Performance & monitoring (Art. 15 / 17)
- Accuracy, robustness, cyber-security

## 5. Conformity assessment & post-market monitoring
</content>
